  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_drai", "name": "Data readiness for AI", "aliases": ["DRAI", "data readiness for artificial intelligence", "AI-ready data"], "description": "The process of preparing and ensuring the quality, accessibility, and suitability of datasets before using them for AI applications; contains a subset of data quality dimensions.", "grounding_span": "Data readiness for artificial intelligence (AI) refers to the critical process of preparing and ensuring the quality,", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_data_quality", "name": "Data quality", "aliases": ["data quality dimensions"], "description": "Traditional dimensions of dataset quality such as completeness, consistency, accuracy, and timeliness; DRAI contains a subset of data quality dimensions.", "grounding_span": "Metrics targeting data readiness for AI (DRAI) contain a subset of data quality dimensions including", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_structured_data", "name": "Structured data", "aliases": [], "description": "Data organized with a consistent format that follows a specific structure or schema, e.g., spreadsheets and relational database tables.", "grounding_span": "Structured data is organized with a consistent format and follows a specific structure or schema.", "location": "§3.1"},
    {"id": "c_unstructured_data", "name": "Unstructured data", "aliases": [], "description": "Data including textual, image, and audio data that presents unique challenges in evaluating and ensuring readiness for AI.", "grounding_span": "Unstructured data, including textual, image, and audio data, present unique challenges in evaluating and ensuring", "location": "§3.2"},
    {"id": "c_textual_data", "name": "Textual data", "aliases": [], "description": "A modality of unstructured data assessed with metrics such as lexical diversity, term importance, readability, topic coherence, and bias indicators.", "grounding_span": "Textual data metrics,\nincluding lexical diversity, term importance, readability score, topic coherence, and bias indicators", "location": "§3.2.1"},
    {"id": "c_multimedia_data", "name": "Multimedia data", "aliases": [], "description": "Image, speech, and video data whose readiness is assessed primarily through quality evaluation metrics.", "grounding_span": "In this subsection, we explore the key aspects of DRAI for image, speech, and video data.", "location": "§3.2.2"},
    {"id": "c_data_preparation", "name": "Data preparation", "aliases": ["data pre-processing", "data preprocessing"], "description": "Collecting, cleaning, organizing, and validating datasets to make them compatible with AI algorithms.", "grounding_span": "which involves collecting, cleaning, organizing, and validating the dataset", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_completeness", "name": "Completeness", "aliases": [], "description": "The presence or availability of required data and attribute values in a dataset.", "grounding_span": "Completeness refers to the presence or availability of required data and attribute values in a", "location": "§3.1.1"},
    {"id": "c_outliers", "name": "Outliers", "aliases": [], "description": "Data points that significantly deviate from the typical or expected values within the dataset.", "grounding_span": "Outliers in a dataset refer to data points that significantly deviate from the typical or expected values", "location": "§3.1.2"},
    {"id": "c_mislabeled_data", "name": "Mislabeled data", "aliases": ["mislabels"], "description": "Instances or data points with inaccurate labels; a form of labeling error or inconsistency in the dataset.", "grounding_span": "Mislabeled data in the context of preparing a dataset for AI refers to instances or data points", "location": "§3.1.3"},
    {"id": "c_duplicate_values", "name": "Duplicate values", "aliases": ["duplicates"], "description": "The presence of duplicate or redundant records within a dataset.", "grounding_span": "This refers to the presence of duplicate or redundant records within a dataset.", "location": "§3.1.4"},
    {"id": "c_feature_relevance", "name": "Feature relevance", "aliases": ["feature relevancy", "feature importance"], "description": "Identifying and selecting the most informative features or variables that contribute to an AI model.", "grounding_span": "This refers to identifying and selecting the most informative features or variables that", "location": "§3.1.5"},
    {"id": "c_class_imbalance", "name": "Class imbalance", "aliases": [], "description": "The highly skewed or uneven distribution of instances among different classes in a dataset.", "grounding_span": "class imbalance refers to the highly skewed or uneven distribution", "location": "§3.1.6"},
    {"id": "c_class_separability", "name": "Class separability", "aliases": ["class overlap"], "description": "The degree of similarity or shared characteristics between different classes or categories within the dataset.", "grounding_span": "Class Separability refers to the degree of similarity or shared characteristics between different", "location": "§3.1.7"},
    {"id": "c_discrimination_index", "name": "Discrimination index", "aliases": ["discrimination", "bias index"], "description": "A measure of unfairness or biases encoded in data that may cause discriminatory outcomes in AI systems.", "grounding_span": "Discrimination in data refers to biases that may cause discriminatory outcomes in AI", "location": "§3.1.8"},
    {"id": "c_data_split_ratio", "name": "Data split ratio", "aliases": ["data splitting"], "description": "Dividing a dataset into training, validation, and testing subsets to maximize model performance and generalization.", "grounding_span": "Optimal data splitting in AI involves dividing a dataset into training, validation, and testing", "location": "§3.1.9"},
    {"id": "c_data_point_impact", "name": "Data point impact", "aliases": [], "description": "The measure of the influence or significance of individual data points within a dataset.", "grounding_span": "It refers to the measure of the influence or significance of individual data points within a", "location": "§3.1.10"},
    {"id": "c_correctness", "name": "Correctness", "aliases": [], "description": "The degree of accuracy and fidelity with which recorded data values align with actual values (the ground truth).", "grounding_span": "correctness refers to the degree of accuracy and fidelity in representing", "location": "§3.1.11"},
    {"id": "c_timeliness", "name": "Timeliness", "aliases": ["currency"], "description": "How closely data captures the most relevant information available at the time of analysis or model training.", "grounding_span": "The timeliness of data refers to the time data collection and its relevance to the phenomenon or", "location": "§3.1.12"},
    {"id": "c_privacy_leakage", "name": "Privacy leakage", "aliases": ["data privacy"], "description": "Protecting sensitive information in datasets from the risk of unauthorized disclosure or inference of private details.", "grounding_span": "Data privacy in the context of AI refers to protecting and preserving sensitive information", "location": "§3.1.13"},
    {"id": "c_sample_size", "name": "Sample size", "aliases": [], "description": "The number of data points or instances selected from a population for training an AI model.", "grounding_span": "Sample size refers to the number of data points or instances selected from a population to be", "location": "§3.1.14"},
    {"id": "c_fair_compliance", "name": "FAIR principle compliance", "aliases": ["FAIR compliance", "FAIR score"], "description": "The degree to which a dataset adheres to Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, and Reusability principles.", "grounding_span": "FAIR compliance of a dataset for AI refers to the degree to which a dataset", "location": "§3.1.15"},
    {"id": "c_lexical_diversity", "name": "Lexical diversity", "aliases": [], "description": "The richness, variety, and complexity of the vocabulary used within text.", "grounding_span": "Lexical diversity measures the richness, variety, and complexity of the vocabulary used", "location": "§3.2.1.1"},
    {"id": "c_term_importance", "name": "Term importance", "aliases": [], "description": "Evaluation of the significance of individual terms in textual data.", "grounding_span": "Term importance evaluates the significance of individual terms in textual data.", "location": "§3.2.1.2"},
    {"id": "c_readability_score", "name": "Readability score", "aliases": [], "description": "A quantitative metric used to assess textual data complexity and ease of understanding.", "grounding_span": "Readability score is a quantitative metric used to assess textual data complexity and ease", "location": "§3.2.1.3"},
    {"id": "c_topic_coherence", "name": "Topic coherence", "aliases": [], "description": "Assessment of the logical and semantic connectedness within a set of topics or a document.", "grounding_span": "This measure evaluates the readiness of textual data for AI by assessing the logical and", "location": "§3.2.1.4"},
    {"id": "c_bias_indicator", "name": "Bias indicator", "aliases": [], "description": "A measure that quantifies and identifies potential biases in textual data.", "grounding_span": "A bias indicator is a measure used to prepare textual data for AI by quantifying and identifying", "location": "§3.2.1.5"},
    {"id": "c_image_quality", "name": "Image quality", "aliases": [], "description": "The degree to which an image accurately represents the visual content it intends to depict.", "grounding_span": "image quality refers to the degree", "location": "§3.2.2.1"},
    {"id": "c_speech_quality", "name": "Speech quality", "aliases": [], "description": "The overall perceived clarity, intelligibility, and fidelity of speech signals.", "grounding_span": "Speech quality refers to the overall perceived clarity, intelligibility, and fidelity of speech", "location": "§3.2.2.2"},
    {"id": "c_video_quality", "name": "Video quality", "aliases": [], "description": "The overall visual fidelity and perceptual coherence of a video sequence.", "grounding_span": "video quality refers to the overall", "location": "§3.2.2.3"},
    {"id": "c_fairness", "name": "Fairness and bias", "aliases": ["fair and unbiased data"], "description": "A pillar concerning the representativeness of data and the absence of biases that could lead to discriminatory practices.", "grounding_span": "Fair and unbiased data is a fundamental aspect of ensuring that AI systems produce equitable and unbiased", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_mia", "name": "Membership Inference Attack", "aliases": ["MIA"], "description": "A technique to determine whether a specific data record was included in the training dataset used to build an AI model.", "grounding_span": "MIA determines whether a specific\ndata record was included in the training dataset used to build an AI model.", "location": "§3.1.13"},
    {"id": "c_synthetic_data", "name": "Synthetic data", "aliases": [], "description": "Data generated to mimic real data while preserving privacy by avoiding the use of actual sensitive information.", "grounding_span": "Synthetic data is generated\nto mimic real data while preserving privacy by avoiding the use of actual sensitive information.", "location": "§3.1.13"},
    {"id": "c_gigo", "name": "Garbage in, garbage out", "aliases": ["GIGO"], "description": "The principle that flawed or insufficient input data leads to inaccurate and unreliable results from AI algorithms.", "grounding_span": "the “garbage in, garbage out” saying, which emphasizes that flawed", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "c_pillar_data_quality", "name": "Data quality (pillar)", "aliases": [], "description": "Pillar ensuring data used to train AI models is accurate, complete, and consistent.", "grounding_span": "Data quality ensures that the data used to train AI\nmodels is accurate, complete, and consistent.", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_pillar_understandability", "name": "Understandability and usability", "aliases": ["data understanding and usability"], "description": "Pillar emphasizing clear documentation, comprehensive metadata, reusability, and accessible data interfaces.", "grounding_span": "Data understanding and usability are important for enabling AI systems to interpret and utilize data effectively.", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_pillar_structure", "name": "Data structure and organization", "aliases": [], "description": "Pillar concerning integrating data into AI workflows via adequate samples, partitioning, schema, and storage organization.", "grounding_span": "Data structure and data organization are important to integrate data into AI workflows seamlessly and efficiently.", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_pillar_impact", "name": "Impact of data on AI", "aliases": [], "description": "Pillar covering the importance of data content and its relevance to AI applications.", "grounding_span": "The impact of data on AI covers the importance of data content and its relevance to AI applications.", "location": "§5"},
    {"id": "c_pillar_governance", "name": "Data governance", "aliases": [], "description": "Pillar for managing data in a way that is ethical, secure, and compliant with legal standards.", "grounding_span": "Data Governance is essential for managing data in a way that is ethical, secure, and compliant with legal standards.", "location": "§5"}
  ],

  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl_poor_quality", "claim_text": "Poor quality data produces inaccurate and ineffective AI models that may lead to incorrect or unsafe use.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Poor quality data produces inaccurate and ineffective AI models that", "location": "abstract"},
    {"id": "cl_140_papers", "claim_text": "This survey examines more than 140 papers published by ACM Digital Library, IEEE Xplore, and journals such as Nature, Springer, and Science Direct.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "This survey examines more than 140 papers published by ACM Digital Library, IEEE Xplore, journals such as Nature,", "location": "abstract"},
    {"id": "cl_jager", "claim_text": "Handling missing values yields up to 20% improvement for classification tasks and 15% improvement for regression tasks.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "They\nobserve up to 20% improvement for classification tasks and 15% improvement for regression tasks", "location": "§3.1.1"},
    {"id": "cl_reviriego", "claim_text": "AI-generated texts often exhibit lower lexical diversity compared to human-generated texts, leading to a feedback loop where AI models trained on such data become less effective over time.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "AI-generated\ntexts often exhibit lower lexical diversity compared to human-generated texts, leading to a feedback loop where AI\nmodels trained on such data become less effective over time.", "location": "§3.2.1.1"},
    {"id": "cl_afendras", "claim_text": "Irrespective of data distribution or analytic task, the optimal training sample size in cross-validation is identified as half of the total sample size.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the optimal training sample size in cross-validation is identified as half of the total sample size", "location": "§3.1.9"},
    {"id": "cl_factor50", "claim_text": "The optimal dataset size for an ANN should be the number of adjustable parameters in the model multiplied by 50 (the 'factor 50' guideline).", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "recommending that\nthe optimal dataset size for an ANN should be the number of adjustable parameters in the model multiplied by 50.", "location": "§3.1.14"},
    {"id": "cl_lack_comprehensive", "claim_text": "There is a lack of a comprehensive study on effective metrics and standards for evaluating data readiness for AI.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "there is a lack of a comprehensive\nstudy on effective metrics and standards for evaluating data readiness for AI", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "cl_quality_linked", "claim_text": "The quality of outcomes generated by an AI system is heavily linked to the readiness of the input data.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the quality of outcomes generated by an AI system is heavily linked to the readiness of the input data", "location": "§1"},
    {"id": "cl_no_unified", "claim_text": "Assessing data readiness for AI presents challenges stemming from the absence of a unified framework that complicates the comparison and consistent application of metrics across diverse dimensions.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "stemming from the absence of a unified framework that complicates the comparison and consistent application of\nmetrics across diverse dimensions", "location": "§6"},
    {"id": "cl_dqt_structured", "claim_text": "IBM DQT and AIDRIN coverage is primarily focused on structured data, leaving a need for advancements to address unstructured data challenges.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "their coverage is primarily focused on structured data", "location": "§6"},
    {"id": "cl_wagner", "claim_text": "Wagner and Eckhoff reviewed over 80 privacy metrics across six domains, categorizing them based on the aspect of privacy measured, required inputs, and data types needing protection.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Wagner and Eckhoff[139] reviewed over\n80 privacy metrics across six domains", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"id": "cl_data_prep_time", "claim_text": "A study involving nearly 2,400 respondents from over 100 countries explains the time-intensive nature of data preparation for data scientists working with AI applications.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "a study [146] involving nearly 2, 400 respondents from over 100 countries explains the\ntime-intensive nature of data preparation for data scientists", "location": "§1"}
  ],
  "instruments": [
    {"id": "i_lof", "name": "Local Outlier Factor (LOF)", "owner": "Breunig et al.", "year": "2000", "method": "Quantifies the level of being an outlier for each data instance by considering the density of the dataset's distribution.", "grounding_span": "Breunig et al . [26] introduce the Local Outlier Factor (LOF) as a metric for identifying outliers in a", "location": "§3.1.2"},
    {"id": "i_ttr", "name": "Type-Token Ratio (TTR)", "owner": "Templin", "year": "1957", "method": "Calculates the ratio of unique word types (vocabulary size) to the total number of tokens in the text.", "grounding_span": "Type-Token Ratio (TTR) introduced by Templin[135] measures lexical diversity in textual", "location": "§3.2.1.1"},
    {"id": "i_tfidf", "name": "TF-IDF (Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency)", "owner": "Ramos et al.; Simha; Qaiser and Ali", "year": "2003", "method": "Combines term frequency and inverse document frequency to quantify a word's significance.", "grounding_span": "TF-IDF (Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency) is a widely used quantitative metric", "location": "§3.2.1.2"},
    {"id": "i_mos", "name": "Mean Opinion Score (MOS)", "owner": "ITU-T", "year": "2018", "method": "Human listeners rate the perceived speech quality.", "grounding_span": "introduced Mean Opinion Score (MOS) [66], which involves human listeners rating the perceived speech quality.", "location": "§3.2.2.2"},
    {"id": "i_ssim", "name": "Structural Similarity (SSIM) index", "owner": "Wang et al.", "year": "2004", "method": "Emphasizes structural similarities in luminance, contrast, and structure to align with human visual perception.", "grounding_span": "Wang et al. [142] introduce the Structural Similarity (SSIM) index as an alternative to traditional error-sensitive", "location": "§3.2.2.1"},
    {"id": "i_psnr", "name": "Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR)", "owner": "MathWorks (reference [8])", "year": "n.d.", "method": "Perceptual quality metric derived from MSE, expressed in decibels, comparing maximum signal power with average squared error.", "grounding_span": "Mean Squared Error (MSE) and Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) [8] are essential metrics for evaluating image", "location": "§3.2.2.1"},
    {"id": "i_ir", "name": "Imbalance Ratio (IR)", "owner": "Alberto et al.", "year": "2018", "method": "Divides the count of majority-class instances by the count of minority-class instances.", "grounding_span": "Imbalance Ratio (IR), introduced by Alberto et al. [12], is a widely used metric to quantify the level of class imbalance", "location": "§3.1.6"},
    {"id": "i_data_shapley", "name": "Data Shapley", "owner": "Ghorbani and Zou", "year": "2019", "method": "Based on the Shapley value from cooperative game theory; measures the contribution of each data point to the model's predictions.", "grounding_span": "Ghorbani and Zou [53] introduced the Data Shapley metric, based on the Shapley value from\ncooperative game theory.", "location": "§3.1.10"},
    {"id": "i_cohen_kappa", "name": "Cohen's Kappa", "owner": "Cohen", "year": "1960", "method": "Assesses inter-rater reliability by calculating agreement beyond chance, ranging from -1 to 1.", "grounding_span": "Cohen’s Kappa [32] is widely employed for\nassessing inter-rater reliability", "location": "§3.1.3"},
    {"id": "i_flesch", "name": "Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level", "owner": "Flesch", "year": "1986", "method": "Estimates the education (grade) level needed to comprehend text using average words per sentence and syllables per word.", "grounding_span": "The Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level, introduced by Flesch [51] estimates the approximate", "location": "§3.2.1.3"},
    {"id": "i_coleman_liau", "name": "Coleman-Liau Index", "owner": "Coleman and Liau", "year": "1975", "method": "Calculates grade level based on average number of letters and sentences per 100 words.", "grounding_span": "The Coleman-Liau Index, developed by Coleman and Liau [33], is another readability scoring method", "location": "§3.2.1.3"},
    {"id": "i_gunning_fog", "name": "Gunning Fog Index", "owner": "Robert Gunning Associates", "year": "n.d.", "method": "Considers both the percentage of complex words and the sentence length, generating a score between 0 and 20.", "grounding_span": "the Gunning Fog Index introduced by Robert Gunning Associates [5] offers an additional perspective", "location": "§3.2.1.3"},
    {"id": "i_cv_coherence", "name": "CV coherence score", "owner": "Röder et al.", "year": "2015", "method": "Quantifies topic coherence by assessing semantic similarity between words within a topic using Latent Dirichlet Allocation.", "grounding_span": "propose the “CV coherence score” [117] to quantify topic coherence in textual", "location": "§3.2.1.4"},
    {"id": "i_asr", "name": "Attack Success Rate (ASR)", "owner": "Carlini et al.", "year": "2022", "method": "Measures the success of an attack in predicting if a specific example is part of the training dataset.", "grounding_span": "Carlini et al. [27] introduced the Attack Success Rate (ASR) as a metric for privacy leakage.", "location": "§3.1.13"},
    {"id": "i_levenshtein", "name": "Levenshtein distance metric", "owner": "Levenshtein", "year": "1965", "method": "Measures the number of edit operations needed to transform one string into another.", "grounding_span": "the Levenshtein distance metric [80]. This metric\nmeasures the number of operations needed to transform one string into another", "location": "§3.1.4"},
    {"id": "i_soundex", "name": "Soundex algorithm", "owner": "Russell", "year": "1922", "method": "A phonetic coding scheme that detects duplicates by comparing the phonetic similarity of character strings.", "grounding_span": "The Soundex algorithm, introduced by Russell [120], is a phonetic coding scheme used to detect duplicates", "location": "§3.1.4"},
    {"id": "i_pesq", "name": "Perceptual Evaluation of Speech Quality (PESQ)", "owner": "Rix et al.", "year": "2001", "method": "Objectively quantifies the quality of processed or degraded speech signals using a model simulating human auditory perception.", "grounding_span": "Perceptual Evaluation of Speech Quality (PESQ), introduced by Rix et al.\n[114], objectively quantifies the quality of processed or degraded speech signals.", "location": "§3.2.2.2"},
    {"id": "i_vmaf", "name": "VMAF (Video Multimethod Assessment Fusion)", "owner": "Netflix", "year": "2017", "method": "Combines traditional metrics like PSNR and SSIM with machine learning techniques to predict perceived video quality.", "grounding_span": "VMAF (Video Multimethod Assessment\nFusion) introduced by Netflix [23]", "location": "§3.2.2.3"}
  ],
  "measures": [
    {"id": "m_fair_14", "text": "The framework includes 14 universal metrics corresponding to specific sub-principles, covering aspects such as identifier schemes, metadata accessibility, findability, access protocols, metadata longevity, knowledge representation languages, linking, adherence to standards, and provenance.", "response_type": "quantitative_metric_set", "grounding_span": "The framework includes 14 universal metrics corresponding to specific\nsub-principles", "location": "§3.1.15"},
    {"id": "m_label_purity", "text": "A label purity metric to measure the impact of adding random noise on the performance of a classifier trained on the dataset.", "response_type": "score", "grounding_span": "introduces a label purity metric to measure the\nimpact of adding random noise on the performance of a classifier trained on the dataset.", "location": "§3.1.3"},
    {"id": "m_aindo_privacy_score", "text": "Aindo AI's privacy score assesses the privacy risk of synthetic data by comparing proximity ratios between real and synthetic datasets; a score of 100 indicates minimal privacy risk.", "response_type": "score_0_100", "grounding_span": "Aindo AI’s [3] privacy score assesses the privacy risk of synthetic data by\ncomparing proximity ratios between real and synthetic datasets.", "location": "§3.1.13"}
  ],
  "standards": [
    {"id": "s_fair", "name": "FAIR Principles", "version": "Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, Reusability", "steward": "GO FAIR", "as_of_date": "2024", "grounding_span": "adheres to Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, and Reusability (FAIR) principles [2]", "location": "§3.1.15"},
    {"id": "s_gdpr", "name": "GDPR", "version": "Regulation (EU) 2016/679", "steward": "European Parliament and Council of the European Union", "as_of_date": "2016", "grounding_span": "non-compliance with regulations like GDPR [48]", "location": "§3.1.13"},
    {"id": "s_itu_p808", "name": "ITU-T Recommendation P.808 (Mean Opinion Score)", "version": "P.808", "steward": "International Telecommunication Union", "as_of_date": "2018", "grounding_span": "The ITU-T (International Telecommunication Union – Telecommunication Standardization\nSector) introduced Mean Opinion Score (MOS) [66]", "location": "§3.2.2.2"},
    {"id": "s_odg_bs1387", "name": "Objective Difference Grade (ODG / BS.1387)", "version": "BS.1387", "steward": "International Telecommunication Union", "as_of_date": "n.d.", "grounding_span": "Objective Difference Grade\n(ODG). STOI, introduced by Taal et al.", "location": "§3.2.2.2"}
  ],
  "frameworks": [
    {"id": "f_aidrin", "name": "AI Data Readiness Inspector (AIDRIN)", "owner": "Hiniduma et al.", "year": "2024", "grounding_span": "AI Data\nReadiness Inspector (AIDRIN) [60].", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_dqt", "name": "IBM Data Quality Toolkit (DQT)", "owner": "IBM / Gupta et al.", "year": "2021", "grounding_span": "IBM’s Data Quality Toolkit (DQT)[44] emphasizes automated\nexplanations of data quality across various dimensions", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_dqlearn", "name": "DQLearn", "owner": "Shrivastava et al.", "year": "2020", "grounding_span": "DQLearn [127] is another tool in this category, focusing on systematically addressing data quality issues", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_deequ", "name": "Deequ", "owner": "Schelter et al.", "year": "2018", "grounding_span": "Deequ [123] stands out as a library that allows\nunit tests for data", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_piq", "name": "PyTorch Image Quality (PIQ)", "owner": "Kastryulin et al.", "year": "2019", "grounding_span": "the PIQ [74] [75] library offers\nan extensive list of metrics", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_dnp", "name": "Data Nutrition Project (DNP) Label", "owner": "Holland et al.", "year": "2018", "grounding_span": "the Data Nutrition Project (DNP) Label [61] provides\na standardized format for presenting essential dataset information", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_drr", "name": "Data Readiness Report", "owner": "Afzal et al.", "year": "2020", "grounding_span": "The Data Readiness Report [ 10] focuses on\ngenerating detailed documentation to assist in data preprocessing for machine learning", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_aif360", "name": "AI Fairness 360 toolkit", "owner": "IBM / Bellamy et al.", "year": "2018", "grounding_span": "IBM’s AI fairness 360 toolkit [46] is an open-source software aimed at detecting and mitigating\nbiases in machine learning models", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_fair_cookbook", "name": "FAIR Cookbook", "owner": "Rocca-Serra et al.", "year": "2023", "grounding_span": "tools like FAIR Cookbook [116] and FAIRassist [49] guide users in implementing and measuring FAIR\nprinciples", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_ess_dive", "name": "ESS-DIVE", "owner": "Cholia et al. / Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory", "year": "2024", "grounding_span": "ESS-DIVE [47] evaluates FAIR compliance in earth sciences data repositories.", "location": "§4"},
    {"id": "f_informatica", "name": "Informatica data quality tool", "owner": "Informatica", "year": "n.d.", "grounding_span": "frameworks like Informatica’s data quality tool [65], an open-source solution\nfor data profiling, cleansing, and monitoring", "location": "§4"}
  ],
  "edges": [
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "d_drai", "grounding_span": "Data readiness for artificial intelligence (AI) refers to the critical process of preparing and ensuring the quality,", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "d_structured_data", "grounding_span": "Structured data is organized with a consistent format and follows a specific structure or schema.", "location": "§3.1"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "d_completeness", "grounding_span": "Completeness refers to the presence or availability of required data and attribute values in a", "location": "§3.1.1"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "d_outliers", "grounding_span": "Outliers in a dataset refer to data points that significantly deviate from the typical or expected values", "location": "§3.1.2"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "d_class_imbalance", "grounding_span": "class imbalance refers to the highly skewed or uneven distribution", "location": "§3.1.6"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "d_fair", "grounding_span": "FAIR compliance of a dataset for AI refers to the degree to which a dataset", "location": "§3.1.15"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "d_lexical_diversity", "grounding_span": "Lexical diversity measures the richness, variety, and complexity of the vocabulary used", "location": "§3.2.1.1"},
    {"type": "defines", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "d_speech_quality", "grounding_span": "Speech quality refers to the overall perceived clarity, intelligibility, and fidelity of speech", "location": "§3.2.2.2"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "c_drai", "grounding_span": "data readiness for AI (DRAI)", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "c_data_quality", "grounding_span": "This distinction between DRAI and data quality is critical for", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "c_structured_data", "grounding_span": "covering\nboth structured and unstructured data dimensions", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "c_unstructured_data", "grounding_span": "readiness of unstructured data, including textual, multimedia, image, speech, and video-related data", "location": "§3"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "c_privacy_leakage", "grounding_span": "privacy\nleakage evaluation", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "c_mia", "grounding_span": "Membership Inference Attacks (MIA)", "location": "§3.1.13"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "c_synthetic_data", "grounding_span": "the use of synthetic data", "location": "§3.1.13"},
    {"type": "mentions", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "c_gigo", "grounding_span": "the “garbage in, garbage out” saying", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "cl_poor_quality", "grounding_span": "Poor quality data produces inaccurate and ineffective AI models that", "location": "abstract"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "cl_140_papers", "grounding_span": "This survey examines more than 140 papers published by ACM Digital Library, IEEE Xplore, journals such as Nature,", "location": "abstract"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "cl_jager", "grounding_span": "They\nobserve up to 20% improvement for classification tasks and 15% improvement for regression tasks", "location": "§3.1.1"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "cl_reviriego", "grounding_span": "AI-generated\ntexts often exhibit lower lexical diversity compared to human-generated texts", "location": "§3.2.1.1"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "cl_factor50", "grounding_span": "the optimal dataset size for an ANN should be the number of adjustable parameters in the model multiplied by 50.", "location": "§3.1.14"},
    {"type": "asserts", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "cl_lack_comprehensive", "grounding_span": "there is a lack of a comprehensive\nstudy on effective metrics and standards for evaluating data readiness for AI", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "about", "from_id": "cl_jager", "to_id": "c_completeness", "grounding_span": "handling missing values enhances predictive model performance", "location": "§3.1.1"},
    {"type": "about", "from_id": "cl_reviriego", "to_id": "c_lexical_diversity", "grounding_span": "AI-generated\ntexts often exhibit lower lexical diversity", "location": "§3.2.1.1"},
    {"type": "about", "from_id": "cl_factor50", "to_id": "c_sample_size", "grounding_span": "the optimal dataset size for an ANN", "location": "§3.1.14"},
    {"type": "about", "from_id": "cl_poor_quality", "to_id": "c_data_quality", "grounding_span": "Poor quality data produces inaccurate and ineffective AI models", "location": "abstract"},
    {"type": "about", "from_id": "cl_wagner", "to_id": "c_privacy_leakage", "grounding_span": "reviewed over\n80 privacy metrics across six domains", "location": "§2.1"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_lof", "to_id": "c_outliers", "grounding_span": "the Local Outlier Factor (LOF) as a metric for identifying outliers in a", "location": "§3.1.2"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_ttr", "to_id": "c_lexical_diversity", "grounding_span": "Type-Token Ratio (TTR) introduced by Templin[135] measures lexical diversity in textual", "location": "§3.2.1.1"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_tfidf", "to_id": "c_term_importance", "grounding_span": "TF-IDF (Term Frequency-Inverse Document Frequency) is a widely used quantitative metric\nto evaluate the importance of words", "location": "§3.2.1.2"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_mos", "to_id": "c_speech_quality", "grounding_span": "Mean Opinion Score (MOS) [66], which involves human listeners rating the perceived speech quality.", "location": "§3.2.2.2"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_ssim", "to_id": "c_image_quality", "grounding_span": "the Structural Similarity (SSIM) index as an alternative to traditional error-sensitive", "location": "§3.2.2.1"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_psnr", "to_id": "c_image_quality", "grounding_span": "Peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) [8] are essential metrics for evaluating image", "location": "§3.2.2.1"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_ir", "to_id": "c_class_imbalance", "grounding_span": "Imbalance Ratio (IR), introduced by Alberto et al. [12], is a widely used metric to quantify the level of class imbalance", "location": "§3.1.6"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_data_shapley", "to_id": "c_data_point_impact", "grounding_span": "the Data Shapley metric, based on the Shapley value from\ncooperative game theory. It assesses the impact of individual data points", "location": "§3.1.10"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_cohen_kappa", "to_id": "c_mislabeled_data", "grounding_span": "Cohen’s Kappa [32] is widely employed for\nassessing inter-rater reliability", "location": "§3.1.3"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_flesch", "to_id": "c_readability_score", "grounding_span": "The Flesch-Kincaid Grade Level, introduced by Flesch [51] estimates the approximate", "location": "§3.2.1.3"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_cv_coherence", "to_id": "c_topic_coherence", "grounding_span": "the “CV coherence score” [117] to quantify topic coherence in textual", "location": "§3.2.1.4"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_asr", "to_id": "c_privacy_leakage", "grounding_span": "the Attack Success Rate (ASR) as a metric for privacy leakage.", "location": "§3.1.13"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_levenshtein", "to_id": "c_duplicate_values", "grounding_span": "similarity\nmetrics for duplicate detection", "location": "§3.1.4"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "i_vmaf", "to_id": "c_video_quality", "grounding_span": "specialized metrics specifically designed to assess the quality of video data", "location": "§3.2.2.3"},
    {"type": "subtype_of", "from_id": "c_completeness", "to_id": "c_data_quality", "grounding_span": "a subset of data quality dimensions including\ncompleteness, duplicates, correctness, and timeliness", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "subtype_of", "from_id": "c_textual_data", "to_id": "c_unstructured_data", "grounding_span": "Unstructured data, including textual, image, and audio data", "location": "§3.2"},
    {"type": "subtype_of", "from_id": "c_multimedia_data", "to_id": "c_unstructured_data", "grounding_span": "multimedia DRAI metrics, such as image,\nspeech, and video quality", "location": "§3.2.2"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_multimedia_data", "to_id": "c_image_quality", "grounding_span": "the key aspects of DRAI for image, speech, and video data", "location": "§3.2.2"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_multimedia_data", "to_id": "c_speech_quality", "grounding_span": "the key aspects of DRAI for image, speech, and video data", "location": "§3.2.2"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_multimedia_data", "to_id": "c_video_quality", "grounding_span": "the key aspects of DRAI for image, speech, and video data", "location": "§3.2.2"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_pillar_fairness_bias", "to_id": "c_discrimination_index", "grounding_span": "Metrics such as the discrimination (bias) index[3.1.8], class imbalance[3.1.6], and class separability[3.1.7] are crucial for\nassessing the fairness of data", "location": "§5"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_pillar_fairness_bias", "to_id": "c_class_imbalance", "grounding_span": "class imbalance[3.1.6]", "location": "§5"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_pillar_fairness_bias", "to_id": "c_class_separability", "grounding_span": "class separability[3.1.7]", "location": "§5"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_pillar_impact", "to_id": "c_feature_relevance", "grounding_span": "Feature relevance[3.1.5] and data point impact[3.1.10] serve as\nquantitative measures", "location": "§5"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_pillar_impact", "to_id": "c_data_point_impact", "grounding_span": "data point impact[3.1.10] serve as\nquantitative measures", "location": "§5"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_pillar_governance", "to_id": "c_privacy_leakage", "grounding_span": "Metrics such as privacy leakage[3.1.13] are essential for understanding the extent of potential privacy", "location": "§5"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_pillar_understandability", "to_id": "c_fair_compliance", "grounding_span": "FAIR\nprinciple compliance metrics[3.1.15] can serve in evaluating data understanding and usability.", "location": "§5"},
    {"type": "has_component", "from_id": "c_fair_compliance", "to_id": "c_correctness", "grounding_span": "evaluate and enhance data correctness in diverse projects", "location": "§3.1.15"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "f_aif360", "to_id": "c_discrimination_index", "grounding_span": "detecting and mitigating\nbiases in machine learning models, providing metrics like representation and statistical rates of sensitive attributes", "location": "§4"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "f_piq", "to_id": "c_image_quality", "grounding_span": "for evaluating image quality", "location": "§4"},
    {"type": "measures", "from_id": "f_dqt", "to_id": "c_completeness", "grounding_span": "data quality across various dimensions, including completeness, feature relevance, label purity, and\ndata fairness", "location": "§4"},
    {"type": "uses_measure", "from_id": "f_dqt", "to_id": "m_label_purity", "grounding_span": "Data Quality Toolkit (DQT) introduces a label purity metric", "location": "§3.1.3"},
    {"type": "implements", "from_id": "s_fair", "to_id": "c_fair_compliance", "grounding_span": "adheres to Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, and Reusability (FAIR) principles [2]", "location": "§3.1.15"},
    {"type": "builds_on", "from_id": "f_aidrin", "to_id": "f_dqt", "grounding_span": "Although IBM DQT [44] and AIDRIN [60]\nhave taken the initial steps in addressing this concern", "location": "§6"}
  ],
  "cites": [
    {"type": "cites", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "gupta-data-quality-toolkit", "grounding_span": "Data Quality Toolkit (DQT) [39] provides a suite of tools and functionalities to", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "cites", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "sidi-data-quality-dimensions", "grounding_span": "various\ndimensions of data quality metrics\ndefined by Sidi et al. [128]", "location": "§1"},
    {"type": "cites", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "wilkinson-fair-metrics", "grounding_span": "Wilkinson et al. [145] introduced a comprehensive FAIR compliance measurement framework", "location": "§3.1.15"},
    {"type": "cites", "from_id": "data-readiness-for-ai-a-360-degree-survey", "to_id": "wagner-eckhoff-privacy-metrics", "grounding_span": "Wagner and Eckhoff[139] reviewed over\n80 privacy metrics across six domains", "location": "§2.1"},

  "proposed_relationships": [
    {"grounding_span": "Many metrics mentioned in the structured data section (§3.1) are also\napplicable to measuring readiness of unstructured data.", "suggested_edge": "applies_to", "note": "Structured-data metrics/concepts are reused for unstructured data; there is no schema edge expressing cross-modality applicability of a metric concept."},
    {"grounding_span": "Aggregating the evaluations across the existing and future metrics will result in a comprehensive\nDRAI assessment", "suggested_edge": "aggregates_into", "note": "Individual dimension metrics aggregate into an overall DRAI assessment score — a part-to-composite scoring relationship not captured by has_component (which is not scoring-aggregation)."},
    {"grounding_span": "Outliers can significantly impact AI systems by skewing data distributions and introducing biases that\nlead to inaccurate models and unreliable predictions.", "suggested_edge": "impairs", "note": "A dimension (outliers) negatively affects AI model performance; the schema has no directional impairs/degrades edge between a Concept and AI-system outcomes."},
    {"grounding_span": "SPEC, an extension of the Laplacian Score, introduced by Zhao and Liu\n[150]", "suggested_edge": "extends", "note": "One metric/instrument (SPEC) extends another (Laplacian Score); extends is only allowed between Definition→Definition or Framework→Framework, not between Instruments."}
  ]
}
